title: 第 14 節
en: 2 M. M., History of Ancient Sanskrit Literature, p. 336.

INTRODUCTION. XCV shad. He, as Colebrooke was the first to point out, takes Mahidasa ' to be an incarnation of Naraya;/a, proceeding from Vuala, son of Ab^a,' and he adds, that on the sudden appearance of this deity at a solemn celebration, the whole assembly of gods and priests (suraviprasangha) fainted, but at the intercession of Brahma, they were revived, and after making their obeisance, they were instructed in holy science. This avatara was called Mahidasa, because those venerable personages (mahin) declared themselves to be his slaves (dasa) K In order properly to understand this legend, we must remember that Anandatirtha, or rather Vijvesvaratirtha, whose commentary he explains, treated the whole of the Mahaitareya-upanishad from a Vaishwava point of view, and that his object was to identify Mahidasa with Narayawa. He therefore represents Narayawa or Hari as the avatara of Vii-ala, the son of Brahman (ab^asuta), who appeared at a sacrifice, as described before, who received then and there the name of Mahidasa (or Mahidasa), and who taught this Upanishad. Any other person besides Mahidasa would have been identified with the same ease by Vuvcsvaratirtha with Visrmu or Bhagavat.

A third legend has been made up out of these two by European scholars who represent Mahidasa as the son of VLs-ala and Itara, two persons who probably never met before, for even the Vaish;/ava commentator does not attempt to take liberties with the name of Aitareya, but simply states that the Upanishad was called Aitareyi, from Aitareya.

Leaving these legends for what they are worth, we may at all events retain the fact that, whoever was the author of the Aitareya-brahmawa and the first three books of the Aitareya-ara;*yaka, was not the author of the two coneluding Ara;/yakas. And this is confirmed in different ways. Sayawa, when quoting in his commentary on the Rig-veda from the last books, constantly calls it a Sutra of Saunaka, while the fourth Arawyaka is specially ascribed 1 Colebrooke, Miscellaneous Essays, 1873, II, p. 42.

to A-s-valayana, the pupil and successor of .Saunaka \ These A two names of 5aunaka and A^valayana are frequently intermixed. If, however, in certain MSS. the whole of the Aitareya-ara//yaka is sometimes ascribed either to A.svalayana or vSaunaka, this is more probably due to the colophon of the fourth and fifth Arawyakas having been mistaken for the title of the whole work than to the fact that such MSS.
A represent the text of the Ara;zyaka, as adopted by the A school of A^valayana.
The Aitareya-ara/zyaka consists of the following five A Ara/zyakas :
The first Ara/zyaka has five Adhyayas :
i. First Adhyaya, Atha mahavratam, has four Khaw^as, 1-4.
2. Second Adhyaya, A tva ratham, has four Khandas,, 5-8.
3. Third Adhyaya, Hihkarerca, has eight2 Kha^Jas, 9-16.
4. Fourth Adhyaya, Atha sudadohaA, has three Kharcdas, 17-19.
5. Fifth Adhyaya, Vasam sawsati, has three Khandas, 20-22.
The second Ara/zyaka has seven Adhyayas :
6. First Adhyaya, Esha pantha^, has eight Khandas, 1-8.
7. Second Adhyaya, Esha imawz lokam, has four Kha«Jas, 9-12.
8. Third Adhyaya, Yo ha va. atmanam, has eight (not three) Khawtfas, 13-20.
9. Fourth Adhyaya, Atma va idam, has three Khandas, 21-23.
10. Fifth Adhyaya, Purushe ha va, has one Khanda, 24.
11. Sixth Adhyaya, Ko 'yam atmeti, has one Khanda, 25. Seventh Adhyaya, Van me manasi, has one Khanda, 26.
The third Ara;zyaka has two Adhyayas :
First Adhyaya, AthataA samhitaya upanishat, has six Khatidas, Second Adhyaya, Prarco vawsa iti sthaviraA Sakalyak, has six Kha?idas, 7-12.
The fourth Arawyaka has one Adhyaya :
15. First Adhyaya, Vida maghavan, has one Khanda (the Mahanamni's).
The fifth Ara;zyaka has three Adhyayas:
16. First Adhyaya, Mahavratasya panlaviwsatim, has six KharzJas, 17. Second Adhyaya, (GrivaA) Yasyedam, has five Kharidas, 7-1 1.
18. Third Adhyaya, (Cru) Indragni, has four Khandas, 11-14.
1 M. M., History of Ancient Sanskrit Literature, p. 235.
2 Not six, as in Rajendralal Mitra's edition.

INTRODUCTION. XCV11 With regard to the Upanishad, we must distinguish between the Aitareya-upanishad, properly so-called, which fills the fourth, fifth, and sixth adhyayas of the second Arawyaka, and the Mahaitareya-upanishad \ also called by a more general nameBahv/'/£a-upanishad, which comprises A the whole of the second and third Arawyakas.
zh: 2 穆勒著，《古代梵文文獻史》，第 336 頁。

導論。柯爾布魯克（Colebrooke）首先指出，他將摩訶伊達娑（Mahidasa）視為那羅延（Narayawa）的化身，此那羅延乃源自毘羅（Vuala），毘羅之父為阿跋（Abya）。他並補充説，當此提婆忽然現身於莊嚴的祭祀儀式時，整個提婆與婆羅門的集會（suraviprasangha）皆為之昏迷，但經由梵天（Brahma）的代禱，他們得以蘇醒，並在禮拜之後，被傳授聖學。此化身之所以被稱為摩訶伊達娑，乃因那些尊敬的人物（mahin）宣稱自己乃其奴僕（dasa）。為了恰當地理解此傳說，我們必須記得，阿難陀蒂爾塔（Anandatirtha），或毋寧説是毘耶濕瓦羅蒂爾塔（Vijvesvaratirtha）——他的註疏即以此人為主——乃從毘濕奴派的觀點來詮釋整部《摩訶艾達瑞耶奧義書》（Mahaitareya-upanishad），其目的是將摩訶伊達娑與那羅延相互認同。因此他將那羅延或訶利（Hari）表現為毘羅的化身，毘羅乃梵天之子（ab^asuta），於祭祀中現身如前所述，並於此處被賜名為摩訶伊達娑（Mahidasa），且教授此奧義書。毘耶濕瓦羅蒂爾塔便會以同樣輕易的方式，將任何其他人物除摩訶伊達娑外與毘濕奴（Visrmu）或薄伽梵（Bhagavat）相互認同。
歐洲學者根據這兩個傳說編造了第三個傳說，他們將摩醯提沙（Mahidasa）描繪為毘濕奴（Vīs-ala）和伊多羅（Itara）的兒子，這兩個人可能從未相識，因為即使是毘濕奴派的評論家也不試圖隨意改動《愛多羅奧義書》（Aitareya）這個名字的含義，而是簡單地說明該奧義書被稱為《愛多羅耶奧義書》（Aitareyi），乃源自愛多羅（Aitareya）。

姑且不論這些傳說的價值如何，我們至少可以確認一個事實：無論誰是《愛德頻耶梵書》（Aitareya-brahmawa）與《愛德頻耶阿蘭雅卡》（Aitareya-ara;yaka）前三卷的作者，都不是最後兩卷《阿蘭雅卡》的作者。這一點可以用各種不同的方式來證實。沙耶納（Sayawa）在他的《梨俱吠陀》注釋中，引用最後幾卷時，不斷將其稱為紹那卡（Saunaka）的經文（Sutra），而第四卷《阿蘭雅卡》則被特別歸屬於¹ ¹ 科爾布魯克，《雜篇論集》，1873年，第二卷，第42頁。
歸於阿濕婆拉雅那（A-s-valayana），他是紹納卡（Saunaka）的弟子兼繼承人。這兩個紹納卡和阿濕婆拉雅那的名字經常被混淆。然而，如果在某些手稿中，整部《艾達瑞亞·阿蘭耶卡》（Aitareya-aranyaka）有時被歸於阿濕婆拉雅那或紹納卡，這更可能是因為第四和第五《森林奧義》（Aranyakas）的跋文被誤認為整部著作的標題，而非此類手稿具有其他性質。
阿蘭亞卡（Araṇyaka）的文本，據阿什瓦拉亞那（Āśvalāyana）派採用的版本。
愛達羅耶阿蘭耶卡（Aitareya-araṇyaka）包含以下五部阿蘭耶卡（Araṇyaka）：
第一部《火祭書》（Āraṇyaka）有五個《經章》（Adhyayas）：
i. 第一阿遮耶，《阿他摩訶跋剌多》（Atha mahavratam），有四部堪陀（Khaw^as），第1-4章。
2. 第二部分（Adhyaya），《阿特瓦·拉塔姆》（A tva ratham），有四個分章（Khandas），第5-8章。
3. 第三阿底雅耶（Adhyaya）《呵依格雷加》（Hihkarerca）有八個卡恩達斯（Kha^Jas），第9至16頌。
第四阿底耶（Adhyaya）《阿塔‧蘇達多哈》（Atha sudadoha）包含三個卡爾恰達（Kharcda），第17-19頌。
第五部分（Adhyaya），《瓦薩姆‧紹薩提》（Vasam sawsati），包含三個分章（Khanda），第20-22頌。
第二個《阿若夜迦》(Arazyaka) 有七個《阿地雅亞》(Adhyayas)：
6. 第一阿地耶亞（First Adhyaya），《埃沙般他》（Esha pantha），共有八個坎達（Khanda），第1至8。
第二章（Second Adhyaya）、「此乃諸界」（Esha imawz lokam）一節有四個知識區段（Khaṇḍas），第9至12頌。
8. 第三阿地雅亞（Yo ha va. atmanam）有八個（而非三個）客罕達（Khawtfas），13-20。
9. 第四阿地雅亞（Atma va idam）有三個堪達（Khandas），21-23節。
第十 第五阿讀耶（Adhyaya）〈普盧舍哈瓦〉（Purushe ha va）有一坎達（Khanda），共二十四節。
11. 第六章《何為自我》（Ko 'yam atmeti）有一分篇，第25段。第七章《言語在我心中》（Van me manasi）有一分篇，第26段。
第三部阿蘭耶卡（Araṇyaka）包含兩個阿迪亞亞（Adhyaya）。
第一阿迪亞耶（Adhyaya）《阿他他槃尼沙陀奧義書》（Athata samhitaya upanishat）有六卡帝達斯（Khatidas），第二阿迪亞耶《普拉爾扥·瓦瓦薩》（Prarco vawsa iti sthaviraA Sakalyak）有六卡帝達斯，第七至十二。
第四部森林書（Aranyaka）有一個學習篇章（Adhyaya）：
15. 第一阿地雅耶（First Adhyaya），吠陀·摩訶婆（Veda maghavan），具有一個堪達（《摩訶南尼》〔the Mahanamni's〕）。
第五部《森林書》（Araṇyaka）共分為三個學習篇章（Adhyayas）：
第16條 首篇《大誓言》（Mahavratasya panlaviwsatim）有六個課頌，第17條 第二篇（頸部 GrivaA）《凡此者》（Yasyedam）有五個課頌，7-11。
18. 第三阿地耶亞（Cru）因陀羅‧阿格尼篇，有四個罕達，第11-14頌。
1 M. M.，《古代梵文文獻史》，第235頁。
2 非六部，如羅閣德剌剌‧米特剌版本所載。
導論。 關於奧義書，我們必須區分兩者：一是狹義的艾達瑞亞奧義書（Aitareya-upanishad），它構成第二阿蘭亞卡（Arawyaka）的第四、五、六章（adhyayas）；二是摩訶艾達瑞亞奧義書（Mahaitareya-upanishad），也以更廣泛的名稱稱為婆俱奧義書（Bahv/'/£a-upanishad），它包含第二和第三阿蘭亞卡的全部內容。
